title: Chicken Parmesan
description: For the best chicken Parmesan, try Chef John's version that's pan-fried and then baked, delivering impressive results with a crispier crust than most recipes.
ingredients: salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 cup panko bread crumbs, or more as needed
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour, or more if needed
0.5 cup olive oil for frying, or as needed
0.25 cup fresh mozzarella, cut into small cubes
instructions: Gather the ingredients. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C).

Place chicken breasts between two sheets of heavy plastic (resealable freezer bags work well) on a solid, level surface. Firmly pound chicken with the smooth side of a meat mallet to a thickness of 1/2-inch.
Season chicken thoroughly with salt and pepper. Using a sifter or strainer, sprinkle flour over chicken breasts, evenly coating both sides.

Beat eggs in a shallow bowl and set aside. Mix bread crumbs and 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese in a separate bowl, set aside. Dip a flour-coated chicken breast in beaten eggs. Transfer breast to the bread crumb mixture, pressing crumbs into both sides. Repeat for each breast. Let chicken rest for 10 to 15 minutes.

Heat 1/2 inch of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it begins to shimmer. Cook chicken in batches, so you do not overcrowd the pan, until golden, about 2 minutes per side. The chicken will finish cooking in the oven.

Transfer chicken to a baking dish. Top each breast with 2 tablespoons tomato sauce. Layer each chicken breast with equal amounts of mozzarella cheese, fresh basil, and provolone cheese. Sprinkle remaining Parmesan over top and drizzle each with 1/2 teaspoon olive oil.

Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is browned and bubbly and chicken breasts are no longer pink in the center, 15 to 20 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 165 degrees F (74 degrees C). Let chicken rest briefly before serving.
author: [{'@type': 'Person', 'name': 'John Mitzewich', 'url': 'https://www.allrecipes.com/author/chef-john/'}]

featured_tweaks.text: This chicken parmesan turned out great. Next time , I will use a bit of a heavier hand with the salt and pepper. I may also beat some garlic into the egg before breading with panko. My family really liked us.I'll make it again next time.I may also try brining the chicken for a little extra flavor.
One of my families favorite recipes. Perfection. Followed it exactly. Just did 6 breasts instead of 4.

I didn’t have enough Parmesan cheese but next time I will! Followed the recipe exactly and the chicken was so moist & delicious! I added some Montreal chicken spice and some Italian mixed seasoning (just sprinkled on the chicken in the frying pan! Used homemade pasta sauce, covered with mozzarella & sprinkled with fresh basil! It will be on my list of dinners for sure!

If you get nice fresh thick chicken breasts it makes a huge difference. The only thing I did differently is use Italian style panko and more tomato pasta sauce. I also did a portobello mushroom with the same breading and threw in with the chicken while it baked for 18 minutes. Just so I had a vegetarian option.

reviews.text: Made with thin sliced chicken breasts and whole wheat panko. Everyone loved it.
I made it the exact same way! Smells so good!
Delicious and simple to prepare. I served the chicken with a side of spaghetti noodles.
